variants.description: Fetch all of your existing dashboard list definitions.

schema.name: dashboard_lists
schema.description: List of all your dashboard lists.

children.name: author
children.description: Object describing the creator of the shared element.

children.name: created
children.description: Date of creation of the dashboard list.

children.name: dashboard_count
children.description: The number of dashboards in the list.

children.name: id
children.description: The ID of the dashboard list.

children.name: is_favorite
children.description: Whether or not the list is in the favorites.

children.name: modified
children.description: Date of last edition of the dashboard list.

children.name: name
children.description: The name of the dashboard list.

schema.name: errors
schema.description: Array of errors returned by the API.

schema.name: errors
schema.description: Array of errors returned by the API.

entries.description: Get all dashboard lists curl example
entries.code:
# Set your Datadog site and credentials
export DD_SITE="datadoghq.com"
export DD_API_KEY="<DD_API_KEY>"
export DD_APP_KEY="<DD_APP_KEY>"

curl -X GET "https://api.datadoghq.com/api/v1/dashboard/lists/manual" \
-H "DD-API-KEY: ${DD_API_KEY}" \
-H "DD-APPLICATION-KEY: ${DD_APP_KEY}" \
-H "Accept: application/json"

entries.code:
"""
Get all dashboard lists returns "OK" response
"""

from datadog_api_client import ApiClient, Configuration
from datadog_api_client.v1.api.dashboard_lists_api import DashboardListsApi

configuration = Configuration()
with ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
    api_instance = DashboardListsApi(api_client)
    response = api_instance.list_dashboard_lists()

    print(response)

# Get all dashboard lists returns "OK" response

require "datadog_api_client"
api_instance = DatadogAPIClient::V1::DashboardListsAPI.new
p api_instance.list_dashboard_lists()
